text:
Simone Biles continued her Olympic redemption story by claiming her second gymnastics gold in Paris and sixth overall.

Biles helped Team USA pick up gold in a team event earlier this week and continue her impressive form in the individual all-around final on Thursday evening.

The American won this title back at Rio in 2016 and was favourite in Tokyo five years later but pulled out after suffering from the ‘twisties’.

Biles produced a sensational display in the Paris event, becoming the oldest winner of her sport’s blue-riband event for 72 years.

After beating Brazil’s Rebeca Andrade and defending champion Sunisa Lee to gold, Biles showed off a new necklace – a sparkly goat in reference to her tag as the greatest of all time.

‘A lot of people love calling me the GOAT, they always do it, so I thought it would be really special if I got one made,’ Biles toldEurosportwhen asked about her new piece of jewellery.

‘The haters hate it so I love it even more. It’s just a special part of me that I have him, as well as a stuffed goat back at the village, just as a reminder to me that I can do this and I’ve done it before.’

Biles finished with an overall score of 59.131, with Brazil’s Rebeca Andrade taking silver and defending champion Sunisa Lee.

‘Oh my gosh I am ecstatic with my performances,’ Biles told Eurosport. ‘It was all pretty good in my opinion, I couldn’t have asked for more.

‘I was a little worried about Rebeca, we were neck and neck basically the whole way through and she’s a phenomenal gymnast. I’m glad she was there to push me.’

Reacting to Biles’ latest gold-winning performance, BBC commentator Matt Baker said: ‘What a comeback, wow.

‘That was absolutely incredible – on so many levels. Simone Biles is back where she belongs. Absolutely another level.

‘You can feel the admiration and respect from everyone here – that is the way to do it. Goodness me it wasn’t easy, it had its ups-and-downs, but surely that is your champion.’

Terrifyingly, for her rivals at least, Biles could yet add three more golds to her collection ahead of three individual finals.

She is due to take part in the vault final on Saturday and the beam and floor finals on Monday.

Biles holds the record for most women’s all-around world titles and most world championship medals.

Before Paris 2024 she was already the most decorated gymnast with 37 world medals.

title: Simone Biles explains brilliant new necklace after winning Olympic gold | Metro News
description: Simone Biles has explained the meaning behind the brilliant necklace she showed off after winning another gold medal at the Paris Olympics.
